title: Tanacetum cinereum
intro: Tanacetum cinereum, also known as Grey Pyrethrum, is a perennial herbaceous plant species in the sunflower family that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the severity of fever. The bioactive constituents of Tanacetum cinereum include sesquiterpene lactones, particularly cinerolone and pyrethrosin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tanacetum cinereum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Tanacetum cinereum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This perennial herb is native to Western Australia and is known for its small, white or yellow daisy-like flowers. Tanacetum cinereum is often found growing in dry areas, and it can be used as a groundcover or in rock gardens. The plant is also known as straggly button or grey daisy bush. It is often used as a low-maintenance ornamental plant due to its ability to thrive in dry conditions.
uses: Tanacetum cinereum is known for its potential medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and digestive issues. Some studies suggest that the plant may possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. It has also been used in herbal remedies for its supposed antiseptic and antispasmodic properties. However, its use in modern medicine is largely anecdotal and requires further scientific investigation.

parts: Tanacetum cinereum is primarily known for its use in herbal medicine, where the flowers are considered the medicinal part. side_effects: Tanacetum cinereum has not been thoroughly studied for its side effects, but like other members of the daisy family, it may cause allergic reactions or skin irritation in some individuals.
